channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: As the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline looms, here are the main sticking points
item.description: The status of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear enrichment program are among the biggest obstacles to extending the truce, which expires Wednesday evening.
item.content:encoded:
The status of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear enrichment program are among the biggest obstacles to extending the truce, which expires Wednesday evening.

(Image credit: Rebecca Conway)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
item.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.
item.content:encoded:
The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends Wednesday
item.description: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends Wednesday. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.
item.content:encoded: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends Wednesday. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
item.description: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.
item.content:encoded: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: She raised concerns about her company's contracts with ICE. Then she lost her job
item.description: Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.
item.content:encoded:
Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: The surprising origin of 4 features that superglue kids — and adults — to screens
item.description: Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.
item.content:encoded:
Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.

(Image credit: Paige Stampatori for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Michaeleen Doucleff

item.title: 3 things to know about Trump's pick to head the Fed as his hearing gets underway
item.description: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve goes before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve goes before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.

(Image credit: Tierney L. Cross)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
item.description: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.
item.content:encoded:
A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

(Image credit: Eduardo Verdugo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
item.description: Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports
item.description: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.
item.content:encoded:
The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

(Image credit: Keisuke Hosojima/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
